text.title: fco-commitment-pricing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for fco-commitment-pricing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Purchasing 3-year Standard RIs for 100% of current capacity without accounting for workload evolution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Workloads shrink due to rightsizing, efficiency improvements, or architectural changes. Over-committed Standard RIs cannot be easily exited; losses can total tens of thousands per month for large environments.

failure-mode.description: Committing before rightsizing — purchasing RIs for oversized instances.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: You lock in the waste. Rightsize first, validate performance, then commit to the correct smaller instance. Committing to m5.2xlarge when m5.large is sufficient means paying 3-year committed prices for twice the capacity you need.

failure-mode.description: Treating RI coverage as a one-time purchase decision reviewed annually.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cloud environments change constantly. Quarterly RI reviews catch misalignments before they become expensive. An RI purchased 18 months ago for a service that was rightsized 6 months ago is wasting money daily.
